description:
You are working as a utilities-script developer who has just joined a project.  
Your very first step is to drop a placeholder script into a standard location so that the rest of the team can hook their tooling to it.  
Complete the following in one single terminal command (you may chain sub-commands with “&&” or “;” if you wish, but everything must be typed and executed as one line):

1. Make sure a directory called “/home/user/scripts” exists. Create it if it is missing.  
2. Inside that directory, create an **empty** file named exactly “archive_cleanup.sh”.  
3. Give that file executable permissions for its owner and read/execute permissions for group and others (octal mode 755).  
4. Immediately after the file is created, write a **single-line** confirmation log to “/home/user/scripts_creation.log”.  
   • The log must contain precisely (no extra spaces, no newline at the start, exactly one trailing newline at the end):
     archive_cleanup.sh: OK

The automated grader will verify the end state by checking:

• Directory “/home/user/scripts” exists and is a directory.  
• File “/home/user/scripts/archive_cleanup.sh” exists, is empty (size = 0 bytes), and its mode is 755 (rwxr-xr-x).  
• File “/home/user/scripts_creation.log” exists and its contents are **exactly** the single line specified above followed by a newline character.

Reminder: perform all four actions with just one terminal command line. No other files or directories should be modified or created.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. Directory: /home/user/scripts
   • Type: directory
   • Permissions: at least 755 (the default on mkdir); group/other write NOT required.

2. File: /home/user/scripts/archive_cleanup.sh
   • Type: regular file
   • Size: 0 bytes
   • Mode: 755 (decimal 493, string rwxr-xr-x)

3. File: /home/user/scripts_creation.log
   • Type: regular file
   • Size: 21 bytes (including trailing newline)
   • Content (shown with ␤ for newline):  
     archive_cleanup.sh: OK␤

No other files anywhere under /home/user should be changed or created by the agent.
